describes: The executable conformance test-case catalog referenced by spec §10.3. Machine-readable twin of SUITE.md (same case ids). A runner in any language drives these cases against vectors/vectors.json (byte-exact KAT inputs) plus the inline self-contained constructions here.

outcomes.match: known-answer test: recompute the operation over the fixed input; result MUST equal the committed expected value
outcomes.accept: validation predicate: a conforming node MUST accept the input
outcomes.reject: validation predicate: a conforming node MUST reject; error_code names the §21 registry code and its Action disposition applies

status_values.vectored: byte-backed by a named entry in vectors/vectors.json
status_values.construction-todo: byte-exact construction recipe given; vector still to be generated as the subsystem gains a fixed-input KAT

notes: reject cases map to spec §21 (104-code registry); error_code + error_name + disposition (Action column) are normative.
self-contained CBOR reject cases assert a deterministic-CBOR (RFC 8949 §4.2 / §18.1.1) decoder rejects the given bytes.
construction-todo cases are non-deterministic to seal (sealed Envelope/Payload) or belong to subsystems not yet vectored (README 'Coverage vs. deferred').

cases.checks: decode(encode(Identity)) is byte-identical; integer-keyed map, ascending keys
DeviceCert deterministic re-encode is byte-identical
Payload deterministic re-encode is byte-identical
Envelope deterministic re-encode is byte-identical
reject non-shortest (non-preferred) integer encoding

input.derivation: uint 23 encoded in two bytes (major 0, ai 24, 0x17); preferred form is single byte 0x17

cases.checks: reject indefinite-length item

input.derivation: indefinite-length array (major 4, ai 31) closed by break 0xff

cases.checks: reject map whose keys are not bytewise-ascending

input.derivation: map {2:0, 1:0}; keys 2 then 1 are descending, violating deterministic key order

cases.checks: reject map with a duplicate key
reject any floating-point value

input.derivation: half-precision float 1.5 (major 7, ai 25); no floats appear in DMTAP wire objects

cases.checks: reject CBOR undefined (and NaN/Infinity/undefined-tag)

input.derivation: simple(23) = undefined (major 7, ai 23); MUST NOT appear

cases.checks: reject a wire map carrying an optional key whose value is null (absent optional MUST be omitted, never null)

input.construction: take vector cbor_envelope, insert key 5 (epoch) => 0xf6 (null) in sorted position, re-encode

cases.checks: a decoder of a signed object rejects any unknown integer key >= 64 (fail closed; keeps the signing preimage unambiguous)

input.construction: take vector cbor_payload, insert key 64 (0x1840) => 0 in sorted position, re-encode

cases.checks: id = 0x1e || BLAKE3-256(bytes), empty input
content address of a 5-byte input
content address of a human phrase
content address of a 4096-byte input
Envelope.id recomputes from ciphertext, before decryption
tampered ciphertext fails the address check; drop before decrypt
deterministic Ed25519 (RFC 8032 Test 1 cross-check)
deterministic Ed25519 (RFC 8032 Test 2 cross-check)
Ed25519 over the domain-separated preimage DS || msg
a valid signature verifies
one-bit message tamper fails verification (fail closed)
one-bit signature tamper fails verification (fail closed)
sender_sig over DS || id || det_cbor(to) || u64be(ts) || u8(kind) || challenge_enc (absent => 0xf6), under ephemeral sender_key
sender_sig verifies under sender_key before any decryption
Payload.sig over DS || BLAKE3-256(det_cbor(Payload minus sig)) under the IK
deterministic 8-word key-name (+ checksum verifies), all-zero key
key-name of all-0x01 key
key-name of all-0x02 key
key-name of a real Ed25519 public key
distinct keys yield distinct names (keyname_key_ones != keyname_key_twos)
a single mistyped word fails the folded checksum (fail closed)
deterministic pair fingerprint / safety number of two identity keys
safety number is order-independent (swapping keys yields the identical value)
unknown suite 0x00 rejected on decode (never guess)
unknown suite 0x03 rejected
unknown suite 0x05 rejected
unknown suite 0xff rejected
known suite id 0x01 (classical) decodes
suite id 0x02 (reserved PQ) is a known id and decodes; an object whose crypto USES 0x02 MUST still fail closed until PQ is implemented
unknown v/suite rejected first, before any crypto
cases.construction: Envelope with v=1 (or an unknown suite)

cases.checks: id mismatch dropped before decryption
sender_sig failure dropped (cheap, pre-decryption)
cases.construction: mote_sender_sig fixture with one signature bit flipped

cases.checks: to that does not resolve to this node/group is dropped
cases.construction: Envelope.to = KeyTag(a key this node does not hold)

cases.checks: cold sender forged/invalid challenge discarded silently, not acked
cases.construction: cold-sender Envelope with a tampered ChallengeResponse

cases.checks: cold sender absent/under-threshold proof deferred to requests area, rate-limited, NOT acked (deferred = no ack)
cases.construction: cold-sender Envelope, challenge absent

cases.checks: ciphertext that fails to decrypt is dropped
cases.construction: Envelope with corrupt ciphertext (id recomputed to keep step 2 valid)

cases.checks: Payload.sig failure => discard silently and do NOT ack (fail closed)
cases.construction: sealed Payload with tampered sig

cases.checks: decrypted from mismatching the pinned identity => warning; MUST NOT silently repin
cases.construction: known-contact Envelope whose Payload.from != pinned identity

cases.checks: suite-ratchet: Envelope.suite below the contact's pinned high-water-mark is a downgrade => requests area + warning; MUST NOT accept, MUST NOT ratchet HWM down
cases.construction: pinned contact HWM=0x02, inbound Envelope.suite=0x01

cases.checks: a duplicate id already held is acked immediately without re-processing
cases.construction: re-deliver an already-stored id

cases.checks: ack owed ONLY for inbox delivery; a deferred cold MOTE is durably held (requests retention 30d, §16.5) but NO ack is sent
cases.construction: cold MOTE deferred at step 6

expect.note: held in requests area, no receipt emitted

cases.checks: a kind the node cannot validate is ignored and MUST NOT be acked
cases.construction: Envelope.kind = 0x40 (reserved, unimplemented)

cases.checks: ts outside +/-120s skew dropped for cold senders (MAY be lenient for known contacts)
cases.construction: Envelope.ts = now + 10 min

cases.checks: an expired MOTE (Payload.expires past) is dropped (cooperative hint, not a security guarantee)
cases.construction: Payload.expires in the past

cases.checks: an Identity whose sig (any suite entry) fails is rejected
cases.construction: cbor_identity with a tampered sig entry

cases.checks: an Identity.version <= the pinned version is rejected (anti-rollback)
cases.construction: pin version=n, then present a validly-signed version=n-1

cases.checks: a broken prev hash chain is rejected
cases.construction: Identity.prev != hash of the pinned prior Identity

cases.checks: KT fail-closed: log unreachable at first-contact pinning => MUST NOT silently TOFU-pin; block or hard-warn
cases.construction: first contact with the KT endpoint unreachable

cases.checks: a DeviceCert with invalid sig, or caps exceeding IK authorization, is rejected
cases.construction: cbor_device_cert with a tampered sig

cases.checks: empty suite intersection => delivery fails closed, no silent downgrade
cases.construction: sender suites intersect recipient Identity.suites = empty

cases.checks: Sphinx packet constant-length on the bucket ladder {2,8,32,64} KiB; wrong length rejected
cases.construction: Sphinx packet off the bucket ladder

cases.checks: MixDirectory verifies under the pinned authority (> n/2 quorum)
cases.construction: MixDirectory with an invalid authority signature

cases.checks: per-epoch replay cache drops a replayed mix packet
cases.construction: replay a previously-seen packet within the epoch

cases.checks: fail-closed no-downgrade: a forced private->fast drop is refused, never silent
cases.construction: attempt to downgrade a private-tier flow to fast

cases.checks: loop-cover / active-attack suspicion is surfaced, not ignored
cases.construction: trigger loop-cover attack detection

cases.checks: a stale MixNodeDescriptor is not used
cases.construction: MixNodeDescriptor past its freshness window

cases.checks: high-security profile and PQ-Sphinx are OPTIONAL (negotiated, not required at Private)
cases.construction: peer without high-security profile / PQ-Sphinx

cases.checks: GroupEvent/GroupState committer_sig verifies under the committer's IK-authorized device key
cases.construction: GroupEvent with a tampered committer_sig

cases.checks: two Commits at the same log position with the same predecessor => committer fork
cases.construction: two conflicting Commits at one position

cases.checks: wrong MLS epoch key selection is rejected
cases.construction: Envelope.epoch referencing a mismatched epoch

cases.checks: Manifest.id = RFC 6962 Merkle root over ordered chunk hashes with domain-separated leaf(0x00)/node(0x01) prefixes
cases.construction: compute MTH over a fixed ordered chunk-hash list

cases.checks: a fetched chunk whose hash != its Manifest.chunks entry is rejected
cases.construction: chunk with a flipped byte

cases.checks: a file routed on the wrong size-tier path is rejected
cases.construction: large file offered on the inline/normal path

cases.checks: a Manifest MUST NOT carry the file key (key rides the sealed MOTE, not the manifest)
cases.construction: Manifest carrying an embedded file key

cases.checks: a gateway attestation that fails to verify under a trusted key is rejected
cases.construction: gateway attestation with an untrusted/invalid signature

cases.checks: invalid DKIM delegation is rejected
cases.construction: DKIM delegation with an invalid signature

cases.checks: a MOTE renders to/from the JMAP object model without loss of the §8-required fields
cases.construction: MOTE <-> JMAP mapping of a fixed mail MOTE

cases.checks: Assertion.sig over DS || BLAKE3-256(det_cbor([rp_origin,nonce,issued_at,exp,aud,cnf])) under the IK-authorized device key
cases.construction: sign a fixed 6-field auth array

cases.checks: an assertion whose rp_origin/aud mismatch the issued Challenge is rejected
cases.construction: Assertion with a mismatched rp_origin

cases.checks: a replayed nonce is rejected
cases.construction: replay a previously-seen nonce

cases.checks: an expired Challenge is rejected
cases.construction: Assertion answering an expired Challenge

cases.checks: the session is bound ONLY to cnf (not the signing key) and MUST reject on cnf mismatch
cases.construction: bind a session and re-present with a mismatched cnf
